summary: Iteration 145: Verified Redis queue migration for route-level background execution. All target route files (email.py, integrations.py, ingestion_engine.py, hybrid_ingestion.py, research.py, marketing_intel.py, file_service.py) now use enqueue_job from biqc_jobs instead of asyncio.create_task(). Conversational routes (soundboard.py, boardroom.py, calibration.py) remain synchronous. All files compile and import cleanly.
critical_code_review_comments: Migration successfully removes asyncio.create_task() from all 7 target route files
New job types registered in biqc_jobs.py: email-analysis, drive-sync, website-ingestion, market-research, file-generation
Conversational routes (soundboard, boardroom, calibration) correctly remain synchronous without enqueue_job usage
Module-level enqueue_job helper properly resolves company_id from payload fields
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 145 validated Redis queue migration for background job execution. Target routes now queue heavy operations (email sync, drive sync, website ingestion, market research, file generation) via biqc_jobs.enqueue_job() instead of asyncio.create_task(). Routes return immediately with queued status when Redis is available, falling back to inline execution when unavailable. Conversational routes remain synchronous for real-time chat experience.

conversational_routes_synchronous.reason: Conversational routes remain synchronous for real-time chat experience
